TextENGNew: "Nfu-fu-fu-fu! There it is -- ron.@k@vS20/11/440700208_01.Chun with three dora. That's mangan."
changeReason: Old translation incorrectly says 'Three chun, three dora'; new correctly parses 中ドラ３ as 'Chun with three dora' (chun is the yaku, three dora are bonus tiles).

TextENGNew: "What...! How does that happen...@k@vS20/19/443100042.How did you end up with three dora?!"
"You fool. You knew Ooishi was in tenpai and you still flipped over another kan dora!@k@vS20/00/443200046.You brought that on yourself."
"After I'd already revealed the chun, stacking dora was the only play left. You can't discard on the dora side after a yakuhai is exposed.@k@vS20/11/440700210.Nfu-fu-fu!"
Ooishi grinned smugly as his grumbling companions broke down the tile wall and began stacking it anew.
``...By the way, Ooishi-san. Identified her yet?``
``What? ...Oh, the mutilated body in the drainage channel.@k@vS20/00/443200048. Any new leads come up?``
``Yes, well. You'll have to find proof yourself, though.``
"Impressive as always. So who was the deceased?"
``Rina Mamiya. Actual name Ritsuko.``
``Apparently she worked for the Blue Mermaid, a hostess bar on Shishibone Flower Road.``
"That's a shady joint. One of the Sonozaki young bosses runs it, doesn't he?"
"...Sonozaki -- whoops! So it's tied to the S-group.@k@vS20/00/442500017....This case is going to be an uphill battle."
"Word is the deceased had been skimming from the Sonozaki family's tribute money. And not a trivial amount, from what I hear."
changeReason: Old translates 上納金 as 'protection money' (extorted from outsiders); new uses 'tribute money,' which correctly reflects internal payments up the yakuza hierarchy.

TextENGNew: "A woman dipping into tribute money... There's got to be a man behind it.@k@vS20/00/443200051.Women rarely pull off that sort of thing alone."
changeReason: Same correction as above: 上納金 is changed from 'protection money' to 'tribute money,' accurately reflecting payments within the yakuza hierarchy.

TextENGNew: "Nfu-fu! He's probably already dead.@k@vS20/11/440700214.The body just hasn't turned up yet."
``About that... Ooishi-san. Her cohabitant's apparently alive.``
"He's alive? Then he'll probably be dead soon enough.@k@vS20/11/440700216.Vermin exterminating other vermin -- now there's a lovely self-cleaning mechanism."
``Anyway, her man...
"It's, well... Teppei Houjou."
``...Houjou? Who's that?``

"...Teppei Houjou. The husband of the housewife beaten to death in Hinamizawa last year.@k@vS20/11/440700218....I'd heard he vanished after the incident.@k@vS20/11/440700219....So he'd been shacking up at that woman's place, had he?"
changeReason: Old translation says 'his lover's place,' implying a romantic relationship; new uses 'that woman's place' (その女の所), which is the neutral/dismissive phrasing actually used in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: "......The Sonozaki stench just keeps getting stronger."
``Where is he now?``
"He was living at the woman's apartment. Apparently he thinks she ran out on him, because he's been making the rounds of bars and hangouts looking for her."
